titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ed Bland Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This collection contains 44 published digital objects.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday February 4, 2026 at 23:49

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection includes manuscript music, notes and writings, correspondence, professional papers, printed material, and audiovisual and computer media documenting the career of African American composer and musician Ed Bland.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 22-27 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 28 (computer media): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Mary Batten Bland on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018, and gift of Mary Batten Bland, 2018, 2021.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Music by Ed Bland, circa 1940-2001. II. Correspondence and Papers, 1949-2011. III. Music by Others, 1914-1966. IV. Audiovisual and Computer Media, 1951-2019, undated.

bioghist.head: Ed Bland (1926-2013)
bioghist.p: Ed Bland, also known as Edward Bland and Edward O. Bland, was an African American musician, arranger, and composer of contemporary classical music. Bland combined his background in jazz with formal training in European classical music to create works ranging from concert music for chamber orchestra, arrangements for commercial record releases, and television and film scores. As a one-time filmmaker, he wrote, directed, and produced the independent documentary The Cry of Jazz (1959), which explored the role of jazz music in African American history. The Library of Congress added the film to the National Film Registry in 2010.

Bland was born on July 25, 1926 in Chicago to Edward and Althea Bland; his father was a self-taught literary critic whose friends included Langston Hughes and Ralph Ellison. He began his career as a jazz musician playing saxophone and clarinet and studied privately under composer John J. Becker before attending the University of Chicago and the American Conservatory of Music. Bland worked as a freelance music producer from 1961 to 1994, composing, directing, and arranging music for commercial record companies and television networks. He composed and arranged pieces for Maya Angelou, Dizzy Gillespie, Lionel Hampton, and George Benson, as well as the PBS American Playhouse presentation of A Raisin in the Sun (1989). Bland also continued to work in film as musical director for Bill Gunn's Ganja & Hess (1973) and orchestrator for A Soldier's Story (1984).

Bland released five albums of concert work, including a compilation of ensemble, bassoon, clarinet, flute, and violin pieces on Urban Classical: The Music of Ed Bland (1994) and Dancing Through the Walls (1998), a dance suite for flute and virtual percussion. His chamber compositions, such as "Piece for Chamber Orchestra" (1979), were performed by the Baltimore, Detroit, Memphis, and St. Louis Symphony orchestras, the Civic Orchestra of Chicago, and the Brooklyn Philharmonic. From 1968 to 1975, he produced over 100 concerts for the Museum of Modern Art in New York. Bland's work was posthumously reinterpreted by pianist Judith Olson on Urban Counterpoint: The Piano Music of Ed Bland (2020).

Bland served as a presidential commissioner for the White House Record Library from 1979 to 1981 under President Jimmy Carter. He was also a member of the National Endowment for the Arts Recording Panel from 1988 to 1989.

Bland was married twice, first to Arlene Wirsing, with whom he had two children, sons Edward, who is deceased, and Robert; his second marriage was to writer Mary Batten, with whom he had a daughter, Stefanie. He died on March 14, 2013 in Smithfield, Virginia.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ed Bland Papers. James Weldon Johnson Collection in the Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing. Information in brackets is also supplied by staff.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes manuscript music, notes and writings, correspondence, professional papers, printed material, photographs, and audiovisual and computer media documenting the career of African American composer and musician Ed Bland. Included are scores and parts for "Piece for Chamber Orchestra" and works he composed or arranged for jazz and funk musicians Dizzy Gillespie, Lionel Hampton, and the Pazant Brothers, filmmaker Madeline Tourtelot, and the PBS American Playhouse presentation of A Raisin in the Sun , as well as a few pieces on which he collaborated with his wife, author Mary Batten. The collection contains papers related to Bland's 1959 film The Cry of Jazz and an unproduced sequel, "The American Hero," his work producing "Jazz in the Garden" for the Museum of Modern Art in New York, and his position as commissioner of the White House Record Library. Also present are extensive notes and essays by Bland on music theory, African American culture, racism, and social justice. Among the correspondents are Black musicians and composers Noel Da Costa, Bunky Green, Major Holley, Ulysses Kay, and Coleridge-Taylor Perkinson, Bland's mentor Henry Sopkin, record executives Neil Bogart and Nesuhi Ertegun, musician and author Sam Charters, conductor JoAnn Falletta, and jazz educator and disc jockey Herb Wong.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Ed Bland Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Commission for the White House Record Library
Museum of Modern Art (New York, N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of autograph manuscript scores and arrangements, annotated ozalids of scores and arrangements, and notebooks of classical and popular music by Ed Bland. Included are songs recorded by Dizzy Gillespie, Lionel Hampton, and Al Hirt, themes for the PBS production of A Raisin in the Sun , and music cues for A Soldier's Story , Second Summer , and other films.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title or folder title, with untitled and unidentified materials filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: "A-2 Car Travel," film cue click track
"Blue Cuchifrito," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie
"Chicken Giblets," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie and The Pazant Brothers
"Chicken Gumbo," arrangement for Al Hirt, GWP Records [ozalid]
"Clabber Biscuits," composition, recorded by the Pazant Brothers and Dizzy Gillespie
"Dance PH 2," with sheet music for "I Dream" and "Lonesome I Walk Along" [ozalid]
"Don't Nobody Mess with My Baby," arrangement
"Don't Nobody Mess with My Baby," arrangement
Film cue for A Soldier's Story [ozalid]
Film cue sheets for various movies
Film cues for Reflections and Here's My Hand
Film cues for Second Summer [Madeline Tourtelot], with shot list and cue lengths
Film cues for To Live Together
"The Fly Fox," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie
Folder of computer printouts of various of Bland's compositions with composer's penciled notes on some [ozalid]
"Fragment for String Quartet" (1949) with composer's notes
"Greasy Greens," composed for and recorded by Lionel Hampton, with poster for performance by Hampton and Al Hirt
"A Gritty Nitty," composition, recorded by the Pazant Brothers
"I Want to be a Woman," lyrics by Mary Batten
"I Want to Be a Woman," lyrics by Mary Batten, music by Bland [ozalid]
"Keep it Greasy," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie
"Loose & Juicy," composition, another version recorded by the Pazant Brothers
"Love Theme" from PBS production of Raisin in the Sun
"Never Gonna Let Him Know," arrangement - Bass part, annotated [ozalid]
"LA Comm'l notes" [enclosed: sheet music for "The Preacher" by Horace Silver"]
"Wind Octet Vol. II" [notebook with exercises and ideas]
Untitled, possibly working notes for "Brass Quintet"
"Notebook I - Sextet," loose sheet music enclosed with notebook
Notes for cues for unidentified movie
"Our Day will Come," arrangement for Larry Paulette album [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Bass part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Bb Trumpet part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Cello part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Clarinet part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Tympani part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Viola part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Violin I part, annotated [ozalid]
"Piece for Chamber Orchestra" - Violin II part, annotated [ozalid]
"Pot Licka," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie
"Rubber Ducky," arrangement for Larry Paulette album Bland produced [ozalid]
"Rutabaga Pie," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie
Samples of score paper used by Bland (blank)
"Sextet for clarinet, trumpet, trombone, tympani, cello"
"Sketches - Set I" - Bb Trumpet part, annotated [ozalid]
"Sketches - Set II" - Bb Clarinet part, annotated [ozalid]
"Sketches - Set II" - Bb Trumpet part, annotated [ozalid]
"Sketches - Set V" - Clarinet piece dedicated to Bill Powell, score
"Sketches - Set V" - Clarinet piece dedicated to Bill Powell, parts
"Sketches - Set VI" (Woodwind Quintet) - Bb Clarinet part [ozalid]
"Sketches - Set VI" (Woodwind Quintet) - French Horn part [ozalid]
"Skunk Juice," composition, recorded by The Pazant Brothers
"Skunk Juice," composition, recorded by the Pazant Brothers
"Tennessee Waltz," arrangement, recorded by Little Rose Little
"Toe Jam," composition, recorded by the Pazant Brothers
"Toe Jam," composition, recorded by the Pazant Brothers
"Turkey Fan," composition, recorded by Dizzy Gillespie
Various handwritten pages - some stapled together by composer, some are film cues
"Violin concerto - for electronic violin and chamber orchestra, dedicated to Janos Negyesy"
"Walter's Theme" from PBS production of Raisin in the Sun [ozalid]
Untitled for GWP Records, may have been for Al Hirt [ozalid]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence, writings, and other papers documenting Ed Bland's work as a musician, arranger, and composer, film director, commissioner of the White House Record Library, and producer of the "Jazz in the Garden" series at the Museum of Modern Art. Included are autograph manuscript notes on music theory, drama, philosophy, and social justice; typescript drafts of articles and essays by Bland about Black music and culture; materials related to The Cry of Jazz and an unproduced sequel, "The American Hero"; and financial records, teaching materials, ephemera, and a small amount of personal papers.

did.unittitle: "African-American Composer's View of African Connections," proof
"Assassins of the Soul," libretto by Bland and Mary Batten, music by Bland
"The Bird's Cage" and "State of Modern Jazz" by Ron Whitney [Bland's pseudonym]
"Black Music as Cultural Warfare," typescript essay
Black Writers of the Chicago Renaissance entry about Bland's father
Composers Reading Sessions, Baltimore Symphony - Bland's piece "Let Peace be Free"
Correspondence with Silas Davis related to payment for arranging Mahalia
Course outline - History of Rock & Roll
Course outline - Music as Politics & Business
Various documents [research material, editorial by Bland]
"Cultural Feedback: African and American Export Loop," paper from Bland's talk during Conference on African Pianism
Cultural warfare essay ["Ed Bland: An Artistic Journey"]
"Essay in dialogue form" about "Black and White Uncle Toms," draft
"Essay in dialogue form" about "Black and White Uncle Toms," draft written in New School creative writing course
"Essay in dialogue form" about "Black and White Uncle Toms," submissions
Interview with Chuck Kleinhaus about The Cry of Jazz
"Jazz is Dead: The Children of the Migrants," book chapter citing Bland
Letters from Ed trying to get work
Lists of people Ed wanted to contact
Music cue sheet for A Raisin in the Sun

Music cue sheets for The
House of Dies Drear
Music, the Arts, and Ideas by Leonard B. Mayer
Plot of Anatomy of Criticism by Northrop Frye
Structural irony in literature, jazz, and Black life
Photograph of Black composers including Bland, Noel Da Costa
Photographs of Bland by Marc Crawford in Los Angeles [photocopied contact sheet]
Police report when [Bland] was stopped on our L.A. street
Project proposal - Approach to musical form
"Report on my Conversations with Schifrin & Others in California"
Titles in Bland's tape cassette and reel-to-reel tape library [notecards]
University of Chicago Repertoire Study Course
"What Do We Need Serious Music For?" [paper written for New School course]
"What is your largest idea?" autograph manuscript notes
"Which problems to solve today," autograph manuscript notes
The White House Record Library , Vol. 2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of music of other composers and study scores, acquired and used by Ed Bland from the 1940s to the 1960s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by composer or author.

did.unittitle: Bach, Johann Sebastian. "Die Kunst der Fuge"
The Chamber Music of Beethoven , edited and devised by Albert E. Wier
The Nine Symphonies of Beethoven in Score , edited and devised by Albert E. Wier
"String Quartet No. 16 in F major, Op. 135"
The Chamber Music of Brahms , edited and devised by Albert E. Wier
The Symphonies of Brahms and Tchaikovsky in Score , edited and devised by Albert E. Wier
Haydn, Joseph. The Symphonies of Haydn, Schubert and Mozart in Score , edited and devised by Albert E. Wier
Krenek, Ernst. Modal Counterpoint in the Style of the Sixteenth Century
Wier, Albert E. (Albert Ernest). Miscellaneous Chamber Works

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains music and films by or relating to Ed Bland.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Audiovisual Media and Computer Media.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains 16mm films, audio and video tapes, and vinyl records. Films include documentaries or educational programs with scores composed by Bland, including To Live Together , a film produced by the Anti-Defamation League about an experimental interracial camp at two Chicago community centers; Youth and the Law , produced by the Mental Health Film Board in New York; Life in a Tropical Forest , a Time-Life production scripted by Mary Batten; and Wooster , which documents a summer filmmaking workshop for children conducted by Bland and photographer Jesse A. Fernandez. Audio tapes feature performances of Bland's music, samples he used to obtain work, and interviews of Bland. Also present are recordings of film cues and music Bland composed for movies and television and commercial record albums he produced.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under five subheadings: 16mm films, Audiocassettes and digital audio tapes, Open reel audiotapes, Videocassettes, and Vinyl records. Within subheadings, materials are arranged alphabetically by title.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 22-28 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Life in a Tropical Forest , music by Bland, script by Mary Batten
did.materialspec: Variable area soundtrack. Composite optical soundtrack. Color print. Faded color. Existing splices. Edge damage and perforation damage. Tears and existing repairs. Eastman date code. Debris in camera gate.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.materialspec: Composite optical soundtrack. Variable area soundtrack. Existing splices. Buckling, tears, perforation damage, opening splices, roping, existing repairs, cinch marks, and staining. Visible frame line are printed in from previous generation. Static electricity marks. Kodak date codes.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.p: All materials consist of audiocassettes and digital audio tapes.
